title: A Conserved Acetyl Esterase Domain Targets Diverse Bacteriophages to the Vi Capsular Receptor of Salmonella enterica Serovar Typhi
summary: The study characterizes a collection of Vi phages targeting Salmonella Typhi, revealing morphological diversity across three families: Myoviridae (Vi phage I), Siphoviridae (Vi phage II), and Podoviridae (Vi phages III-VII). Vi phage I has a complex Myoviridae structure with an isometric icosahedral head (~88-90 nm) and a long contractile tail (~115 nm). The Podoviridae members (III-VII) have icosahedral capsids (~60 nm diameter) with short tails. All phages encode tail spike proteins with an acetyl esterase domain for Vi capsule recognition.

Vi phage I.morphology: Myoviridae morphotype A1, isometric icosahedron-shaped head, complex tail fiber structure
Vi phage I.capsid_size: ~88 nm from apex to tail joint and ~90-nm width
Vi phage I.structural_proteins: capsid, DNA packaging, and neck proteins (Vi01_152c to Vi01_166c), three tail spike proteins (Vi01_170c, Vi01_171c, Vi01_172c)

extra.head_shape: icosahedron-shaped head with visible small extension at each corner
extra.tail_features: collar present, end plate terminating the tail, intricate array of minor tail fibers, four-pronged arrangement of fine tail fibers
extra.tail_contraction: smaller inset image most likely shows the phage after tail contraction
extra.similar_phages: similar to Salmonella phage Det7 and S. Heidelberg phage 10 tail fiber structures, resembles Shigella boydii phage phiSboM-AG3

extra.related_phage: related to S. Typhimurium phage ES18
extra.synteny: shares regions of synteny with the lysogenic phage ES18

extra.genomic_similarity: closely resembles enterobacterial phage K1F, marked similarity to EcoDS1 and T7
extra.tail_spike_features: N-terminal domain with homology to known tail spike proteins (e.g., Gp36 in K1F)

Vi phage IV.structural_proteins: one extra tail spike (Vi04_59), trimeric adaptor protein gp37 homolog (Vi04_45)

extra.genomic_similarity: most clearly resembles S. Typhimurium lytic phage SP6, synteny to Salmonella phage SP6
extra.tail_spike_attachment: tail spike attached by trimeric adaptor protein gp37 (different mechanism from other Vi Podoviridae)
extra.unique_gene: putative S-adenosylmethionine hydrolase gene not present in SP6, K1-5, or K1E

extra.genomic_similarity: most closely resembles T7 phage, >90% homology to Vi phages VI and VII at DNA level
extra.tail_spike_features: N-terminal domain similar to T7 Gp17

Vi phage VI.plaque_morphology: 0.3- to 0.4-cm plaques, consistently the largest and having strikingly clear centers
Vi phage VI.structural_proteins: tail spike Vi06_43, capsid assembly protein Vi06_34, major capsid protein Vi06_35

extra.genomic_similarity: most closely resembles T7 phage, >95% homology to Vi phage VII at DNA level
extra.tail_structure: arrangement of six minor fine tail fibers seen in electron micrograph
extra.mass_spec_identified: tail constituents (part of gp17 of T7), head-to-tail joining protein (gp8), internal virion proteins (gp14, gp15, gp16 equivalents)

extra.genomic_similarity: most closely resembles T7 phage, >95% homology to Vi phage VI at DNA level
extra.tail_spike_features: N-terminal domain similar to T7 Gp17

extra.similarity: Vi phage I tail fiber structure reminiscent of Salmonella phage Det7
Vi phage I tail fiber structures very similar to those reported for S. Heidelberg phage 10

extra.structural_studies: arrangement previously described in structural studies of T7 and K1E phage
extra.genomic_similarity: Vi phages V, VI, and VII most closely resemble T7

SP6.structural_proteins: trimeric adaptor protein gp37, tail spike protein

extra.genomic_similarity: Vi phage IV most clearly resembles S. Typhimurium lytic phage SP6
Vi phage I closely resembles phiSboM-AG3, displaying remarkable synteny
extra.shared_proteins: 170 proteins have homology between Vi phage I and phiSboM-AG3, 72 predicted proteins unique to both

extra.genomic_similarity: capsid, DNA packaging, and neck protein gene cluster in Vi phage I found with variable homology in cyanobacterial lytic phages like P-SSM2
capsid, DNA packaging, and neck protein gene cluster in Vi phage I found with variable homology in cyanobacterial lytic phages like S-PM2; T4 prototypes in Vi phage I closely matched those in S-PM2
significant number of genes with known prototypes carried by T4 phage identified in Vi phage I annotation
Vi phage III closely resembles K1F; K1F is another member of the T7-like phage genus
Vi phage III has marked similarity to EcoDS1
Vi phage IV most clearly resembles S. Typhimurium lytic phage SP6
Vi phage IV compared to K1-5
Vi phage IV compared to K1E; structural studies of K1E phage mentioned
extra.tail_spike_attachment: K1E uses trimeric adaptor protein gp37 for tail spike attachment

extra.related_phage: Vi phage II is a member of the Siphoviridae, related to ES18
